equivalence_argument: The current LLM calculator fails closed for missing/decode/model failures but not for executor-reported has_error responses.

negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: LLM executor outputs that report has_error=true are priced with a constant fee instead of returning Err and causing builder skip/verifier rejection.
related_but_insufficient_code: compute_llm_executor_fee returns Err for missing output, decode failures, and unknown models, but returns Ok(LLM_ERROR_EXECUTOR_FEE_WEI) when decode_llm_response reports LLM_HAS_ERROR_PREFIX; a test asserts this constant-fee behavior.

notes: Confirmed gap limited to executor-side error outputs; other LLM failure modes already return errors.
rationale: The commit message explicitly states strict no-fallback policy, and the new LLM calculator returns Err for missing output, decode failures, executor-side errors, and model resolution errors. calculate_async_fees propagates those errors for the LLM precompile. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: LLM async fee calculation must return an error, not a fallback HTTP or default price, when executor output is missing, cannot be decoded, reports an error, or resolves to an unknown/unpriceable model.
suggested_falsification_tests: Call calculate_async_fees with the LLM precompile and None output, malformed output, output with has_error set, and output naming an unknown model; assert every case returns Err and no HTTP executor fee is substituted.
summary: LLM executor-reported errors still fall back to a constant fee.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Call calculate_async_fees for LLM output with has_error=true and assert the invariant expects Err; current code returns Ok(LLM_ERROR_EXECUTOR_FEE_WEI).
title: LLM pricing failures do not fall back
